finding: No investment-risk disclosure, Key Fact Sheet, or suitability check anywhere in the buy flow. The only risk-related artifact before commitment is a liability declaration on Review. The first-time T&C modal lists four agreements by title only ('Platform T&Cs', 'Precious Metal Account T&Cs', 'Investment Module T&Cs', 'Business Account Board Reso T&Cs') with no openable documents, yet requires the user to click 'Agree'.
novice_impact: Commits to a 30,642.50 SGD order with zero disclosure that precious-metal values can fall. Cannot make an informed decision; agrees to documents they cannot read.
seasoned_impact: Expects a Key Fact Sheet / risk-warning statement as standard for a regulated investment product; its absence reads as immature and creates regulatory exposure.
recommendation: Add a dedicated risk-disclosure step before 'Confirm buy order': a Key Fact Sheet / risk-warning statement with an explicit 'I understand precious-metal values can fall' acknowledgement, and make the four T&Cs in the modal openable (links or expandable text). Separate liability declaration (protects bank) from risk disclosure (protects customer).

finding: Inline validation and error states exist only as isolated spec frames (insufficient balance, below-minimum, exceed-market-value) and are not rendered on the live Buy/Sell trade screen. No error border, no inline message anchored to a field, and validation timing / CTA-gating cannot be confirmed. For a money-moving flow the on-screen error state is mandatory and appears undesigned/uninstrumented.
novice_impact: If validation fires late or silently, a novice can be blocked at submission with no idea why, unable to recover.
seasoned_impact: Slows the expert who expects immediate, field-anchored feedback; erodes confidence in a trading tool.
recommendation: Integrate the spec'd error/warning states into the actual trade screen with field-anchored inline messages, on-blur validation, and a CTA that gates with a stated reason. Specify hover/focus/active/loading states too.

finding: The gap between the displayed 'Indicative price' and the actual execution price is never disclosed. The quoted indicative price is 6,581.85 SGD/ounce, but the completed trade detail shows an actual 'Purchase price 6,128.50 SGD/ounce' — a materially different number. Nothing warns that the price can move between input and settlement.
novice_impact: Perceives a bait-and-switch when the charged price differs from the quoted price; loses trust.
seasoned_impact: Expects price movement but still needs it documented; without a stated convention it's a compliance gap.
recommendation: Add an 'Indicative price' tooltip and an execution caveat at input and review: 'Final price is set at execution and may differ from the indicative price shown.' Show the executed price prominently on confirmation.

finding: Landing marketing copy is upside-only: 'Buy paper bullion to diversify your portfolio, hedge against inflation, and access a globally recognised store of value.' There is no balancing 'the value of precious metals can fall as well as rise' statement before the call to action.
novice_impact: Forms an unbalanced, optimistic mental model of risk before ever seeing a price.
seasoned_impact: Recognises the omission as marketing spin; low practical impact.
recommendation: Add a concise, equally-weighted risk statement adjacent to the value proposition on the landing (not only in a collapsed FAQ).

finding: The only risk-bearing content ('Things to look out for') is a collapsed FAQ accordion, below the fold, positioned after 'Reason to invest' and after the 'Start trading' CTA, and softened with a euphemistic label.
novice_impact: Risk is effectively hidden at the exact moment a novice is deciding to invest.
seasoned_impact: Minor — will seek risk info elsewhere.
recommendation: Rename to 'Risks', surface key risks above the fold or inline near the CTA, and expand by default for first-time users.

finding: Unfilled template artifacts are shipped into customer-facing surfaces: maintenance modal shows '[6:00 AM SGT]' and '[6:00 AM]' bracket tokens (and the two phrasings disagree on timezone); the confirmation email shows 'Dear <Salutation> <Surname>' and a '<Name> / <Designation/rank> / <Department name>' signature plus a stale '© Copyright 2024'; the push notification leaks delimiters '<7,000.00 SGD/ounce>'.
novice_impact: Reads as broken/unfinished; undermines trust in a bank.
seasoned_impact: Same — looks like a QA failure on a financial product.
recommendation: Purge all merge-field/placeholder tokens, fill the maintenance time dynamically with a single consistent timezone format, and correct the copyright year.

finding: Price freshness is not communicated for a live-priced commodity. The overview says 'Last updated 23 April 2026, 11:30 AM (UTC+08:00)' while the trade-panel chart says 'Last updated 28 Apr 2026, 2:00 PM SGT' — a ~5-day discrepancy within the same product. There is no auto-refresh, countdown, or 'price expires in / refresh' affordance.
novice_impact: Cannot tell whether they are transacting on a stale quote.
seasoned_impact: High — price accuracy and freshness are paramount; a stale quote is unacceptable for active trading.
recommendation: Show a single live timestamp, auto-refresh the indicative price with a visible countdown/refresh control, and reconcile the conflicting timestamps.

finding: No loading/processing state exists between 'Confirm buy order' and the success screen. Order submission, price lock, and account creation all involve latency, but there is no spinner, skeleton, or 'Submitting order…' state — risking double submission and user uncertainty at the most critical moment.
novice_impact: May click Confirm twice in uncertainty; high anxiety with no system feedback.
seasoned_impact: Double-submission risk on a real order; expects an explicit pending state.
recommendation: Add an explicit processing state that disables the button, shows progress, and prevents resubmission until the server responds.

finding: The Review screen (pre-commit) and the Trade-detail screen (already executed) are near-identical in layout and content; the only differentiators are the small kicker (REVIEW vs TRADE DETAILS) and the buttons. A user landing mid-flow cannot tell whether money has already moved.
novice_impact: May believe the order is already placed and abandon, or re-submit; serious confusion about transaction state.
seasoned_impact: On a fast scan, still risks misreading committed vs uncommitted state.
recommendation: Visually differentiate the two: distinct headers, a clear status banner ('Not yet submitted' vs 'Completed · Filled'), and a step indicator. Make the pre-commit state unmistakable.

finding: Buy/Sell is differentiated almost entirely by colour (green Buy / red Sell pill) with no icon, arrow, or shape redundancy, and gain/loss values on the Holding-details card rely on green/red with only a weak '+/-' sign. Brand red is simultaneously used for OCBC branding, the Sell state, and loss values — so the most saturated red on the Sell screen is the toggle, not the primary action.
novice_impact: A red/green colour-blind novice can confuse Buy and Sell on a real money transaction.
seasoned_impact: Same accessibility risk; the overloaded red also muddies action priority.
recommendation: Add non-colour redundancy (directional icons/labels) to Buy/Sell and gain/loss everywhere, and decide a single meaning for red (brand vs action vs sell) so the primary CTA reads unambiguously.

finding: Currency context can shift silently. Overview toggles SGD/ounce vs USD/ounce, trade history mixes SGD, USD and EUR rows, and alerts mix SGD/USD. A user can read a price in one currency and transact in another without noticing the unit change.
novice_impact: May misjudge price/value by transacting in a different currency than they read.
seasoned_impact: Manages multiple currencies but still needs an unmistakable, persistent currency indicator.
recommendation: Make the active currency a persistent, prominent indicator across all screens; warn on currency switch; align overview, history and alert currencies or label each row's currency unambiguously.

finding: At the highest-anxiety moment — confirming a 30,642.50 SGD order — the only copy is a cold liability declaration: 'Declarations which are found to be untrue or inaccurate may result in the delay or rejection of your investment, without recourse to OCBC Bank.' There is no reassurance (e.g. 'funds are only debited on settlement', security signal, or 'you can review before it's final').
novice_impact: The coldest copy on the most consequential screen increases anxiety rather than building confidence.
seasoned_impact: Less affected, but reassurance about settlement/debit timing is still valuable.
recommendation: Pair the legal declaration with plain-language reassurance about what happens next (debit/settlement timing, security), and keep the legal text secondary.

finding: The alert list filter counter and the table disagree: the 'All (5)' pill is shown while the table renders 7 rows with different target prices/dates across the two captured states — a correctness/trust bug.
novice_impact: Confusing but lower stakes for a novice who has fewer alerts.
seasoned_impact: High — power users rely on many alerts and on data accuracy; a count/row mismatch breaks trust in the data.
recommendation: Reconcile the filter counts with the rendered rows; treat as a data-integrity defect, not cosmetic.

finding: The primary CTA is weak and buried: 'Review buy order' sits bottom-right below a full-width divider, rendered in slate-grey, and when disabled is nearly invisible with no hint of what's missing to enable it. Meanwhile the visually richest element (the line chart) is decision-support, not the task, pulling the eye away from the form.
novice_impact: May not notice the next-step button or understand why it's disabled.
seasoned_impact: Minor friction locating the action quickly.
recommendation: Elevate the primary CTA (consistent treatment, stronger weight, clear enabled/disabled), and pair the disabled state with a reason ('Enter an amount to continue').

finding: Novice-hostile terms are used without inline explanation: ISO codes 'XAU/XAG' (never expanded on first use), 'ounce' (never stated as troy ounce), 'Indicative price', 'Credit account'/'Debit account' (a buy's 'Credit account - Gold (XAU)' reads as being given credit), 'Order status: Filled', and 'Market value (Indicative)'. The only glossary is the FAQ on the Overview tab — absent from the input, review, detail and sell screens where the jargon actually appears.
novice_impact: Multiple terms scoring 2/5 for comprehension with no help where they appear; high confusion.
seasoned_impact: Comfortable with the jargon; no impact.
recommendation: Add inline tooltips/glosses on first use ('troy ounce', 'Filled = completed', define XAU/XAG, clarify Credit/Debit account), and carry FAQ definitions onto transactional screens via progressive disclosure so experts aren't slowed.

finding: The header/eyebrow system is not standardised: the kicker appears inline top-left on some screens, in the left gutter with a red rule on others, and is absent (only an H2) on the overview. There is no consistent page-title anchor and no step indicator ('Step 2 of 3') in a multi-step money flow.
novice_impact: Weakened 'where am I' orientation through a consequential flow.
seasoned_impact: Minor.
recommendation: Standardise one eyebrow+H1 pattern across all screens and add a step indicator to the buy/sell flow.

finding: The amount-to-quantity swap control (the vertical double-arrow between 'Investment amount' and 'Quantity to buy') has no label, button chrome, hover hint, or tooltip. It's unclear it's an interactive control that switches which field drives the calculation.
novice_impact: Won't understand the two fields are two views of one decision, or that the control is interactive.
seasoned_impact: Will infer it, but the affordance is still ambiguous.
recommendation: Give the swap control a button container and tooltip ('Switch between amount and quantity') and a clear active/driver indication.

finding: Account creation is messaged twice and inconsistently: the buy input says 'New Gold (XAU) Account will be created with your order', while a separate first-time modal says 'a Gold (XAU) account will be created for you' — leaving it unclear when the account is actually opened.
novice_impact: Doesn't know whether the account already exists or when it's created.
seasoned_impact: Minor redundancy.
recommendation: Use one clear, consistent account-creation message at a single, well-defined point in the flow.

finding: Blocking errors (e.g. 'Insufficient balance') and soft warnings share an identical amber/triangle treatment, with no visual distinction between a hard block and a caution. Three banner variants also coexist (tight field banner, large padded container banner, and a page-level banner with a close ×).
novice_impact: Can't distinguish a stop ('you cannot proceed') from a heads-up.
seasoned_impact: Same ambiguity; slows triage.
recommendation: Introduce distinct error vs warning styles (colour/icon) and consolidate to one banner component with size variants.

finding: Field labels and helper text ('Investment amount', 'Quantity to buy', 'Minimum 24.59 SGD') are light grey inset on light-grey fills, with borderline contrast likely failing WCAG AA at ~11px. The empty-state '0.00' is rendered in the same dark weight a real value would use, so a placeholder is indistinguishable from an actual entered zero.
novice_impact: Hard-to-read labels; confusion about whether a value is entered.
seasoned_impact: Minor readability friction.
recommendation: Raise label/helper contrast to AA and visually distinguish placeholder from entered values (lighter placeholder, clear focus/filled states).

finding: The trade-detail screen is a dead end (only 'Back'), with no 'Sell this holding', 'Download receipt', or 'Set an alert' — the obvious next actions. The success screen offers only 'Back to overview' and 'View order' with no 'Place another trade', despite this being a repeat-action product.
novice_impact: Lower impact; fewer repeat actions.
seasoned_impact: High — batch/repeat traders must re-navigate Overview → Trade panel for every order.
recommendation: Add contextual next-actions on trade detail (sell, download, set alert) and a 'Place another trade' shortcut on success.

finding: Number, unit and account formatting drift across screens: 'ounce' (singular) vs 'ounces'; account numbers as '410-212232-201 - XAU' vs 'XAU-410-212232-201' vs '647561400225-SGD' (no hyphens); unit phrasing 'SGD/ounce' vs 'SGD / ounce'; and the card label 'Holdings details' vs 'Holding details'.
novice_impact: Small cognitive friction reconciling the same data shown differently.
seasoned_impact: Detail-oriented users notice inconsistency and question data integrity.
recommendation: Define and enforce one formatting standard for currency units, account numbers, pluralisation, and shared labels.

finding: The 'Back' button on Review is unlabeled as to consequence — it is unclear whether it discards the order or returns to edit inputs. In a money-moving flow, an ambiguous back action is a trust gap.
novice_impact: May fear losing the order or accidentally discarding it.
seasoned_impact: Minor hesitation.
recommendation: Label the intent ('Edit order' vs 'Cancel') and confirm before any destructive discard.

finding: The primary-button system is not systematised: slate-grey is the de-facto primary while OCBC red is never used for a primary action; the same 'Back' action appears as a blue text-link on the success screen but an outlined button on trade-detail and review. Two near-identical confirmation screens also use different backgrounds (grey page + white card vs white page, no card).
novice_impact: Inconsistent affordances make 'what's the action' less predictable.
seasoned_impact: Minor, but reads as low polish.
recommendation: Define one primary, one secondary, and one tertiary button treatment and apply consistently; unify the two confirmation layouts.

finding: Global chrome drifts across captures: the top nav reads 'FX and Investment' on most screens but 'FX and treasury' on a dashboard variant, and some screens show a Search pill and help '?' icon that others lack. Design-spec frames (error banners, alert input) are also mixed into the product set on peach/grey artboards rather than shown integrated.
novice_impact: Negligible per screen; slight inconsistency in the nav label.
seasoned_impact: Negligible.
recommendation: Reconcile to one nav taxonomy and one header chrome; keep spec frames out of the production flow or render error states in-context.

finding: On the success screen the confirming sentence ('Buy order for Gold (XAU) has been filled successfully') is set in lighter mid-grey, smaller than the figures beneath it; the green check carries all the visual weight. The most important message on a success screen should be its strongest text element.
novice_impact: Reassurance message is under-emphasised at a moment that benefits from clear closure.
seasoned_impact: Negligible.
recommendation: Promote the success sentence to the strongest text on the screen (size/weight/contrast).
